commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=62aaae1bf2b30bd50d1d7d785a0a93ccb6809c3c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adjusts Windows-oriented tests and path redaction by sorting bundled channel IDs, pinning respawn tests to Linux, handling symlink EPERM in tests, and normalizing support/trajectory redacted paths to POSIX separators.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +25. Total +56 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings by source inspection: the symlink test can pass on a missing file after EPERM, and normalizeToPosix is applied to generic redactSupportString output. I did not establish the claimed Windows test failures in a real Windows environment during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor-provided Windows proof, a rebase, and patch fixes; it is not a safe automated repair lane because automation cannot produce the contributor's real environment proof.

target.title: fix(windows): resolve compatibility, path redaction, symlink EPERM, and channel sorting issues
target.body:
This PR fixes exactly 5 distinct Windows compatibility and test determinism issues to make the test suite pass stably on Windows.

1. **Deterministic Channel Sorting**: Sorted returned channel IDs alphabetically in package-state-probes.ts to prevent non-determinism.
2. **Robust Symlink Handling in Tests**: Wrapped fs.symlink calls in server.test.ts and backend-config.test.ts in try-catch blocks to gracefully handle and bypass EPERM.
3. **POSIX Path Normalization in Redactions**: Normalized path strings and prefix variants to POSIX format (forward slashes, stripped drive letters) in diagnostic-support-redaction.ts.
4. **Trajectory Export Path Normalization**: Updated workspace path redaction in export.ts to use forward slashes when matching .
5. **Adjacent Test Stability**: Mocked platform: 'linux' in entry.respawn.test.ts and wrapped expected paths in path.resolve in runtime.test.ts.

All tests have been run and verified successfully on Windows.
target.author: aniruddhaadak80

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
